programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: Red and Kitty mark their 25th anniversary; Vic (Bruce Willis) offers Kelso a job at a gentlemen's club in Chicago.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Brad fails to tell the truth about his car accident; three generations of the Al Unser car-racing family visit "Tool Time.".

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: A "Tool Time" mishap may prevent Tim from keeping an important date with Mark and setting a tool show record.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Jill (Patricia Richardson) has trouble dealing with the fact that the boys no longer want to spend much time with her. With Tim Allen.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: "Tool Time" gets approval to create a 3-D effects show; Jill (Patricia Richardson) wants Tim (Tim Allen) to act more like an equal.

programme.title: Bob Hearts Abishola
programme.desc: Bob makes things worse after interfering in Goodwin and Kofo's ongoing spat; Abishola puts her pride on the line when she realizes she's been an inattentive friend to Kemi.

programme.title: Bob Hearts Abishola
programme.desc: As Abishola begins studying for medical school, she offends Gloria with an insensitive comment about Gloria's own journey of almost becoming a doctor; Tunde schools a reluctant Bob on the art of home repair while helping to fix his dishwasher.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: Jim joins Cheryl's yoga class after getting jealous of her male instructor.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: Kyle and Andy both fall for Ruby's piano teacher, Mandy (Jackie Debatin).

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Ed plans a surprise dinner for Mike's 25 years at The Outdoor Man; Mandy wants her and Kyle to move in with her parents to save money after they're married.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: A bear wanders into the store when Kyle leaves the loading dock door open.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Mike learns that Mandy and Kyle's wedding shower is on the same day as the big Broncos-Chargers game; Ryan offers to host the men's party at his place; Ed insists that abiding by his superstitious rituals is the only way the Broncos will win.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Mandy starts vaping, to her parents' disappointment; Vanessa commiserates with a fellow teacher about the impending strike; Ryan and Kristin find a secret safe in the basement.

programme.title: Man With a Plan
programme.desc: Andi accuses Adam of unconscious bias after he only interviews men for a job; Andi helps Kate fight for a spot on the boys' soccer team.

programme.title: Man With a Plan
programme.desc: Adam and Andi try to get back in their neighbors' good graces after no one shows up to their potluck dinner.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: Jim tries to teach Kyle a lesson about quitting by running a 10k marathon, but he ends up cheating in the race.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: In an effort to outdo Cheryl's story about meeting Oprah, Jim makes up a story about his construction office catching fire.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: Jim helps Andy pick up a beautiful woman at the bar, but she turns out to be crazy.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: To teach Jim the value of communication, Cheryl does not speak to him for three days.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Tim (Tim Allen) is uncomfortable discussing personal matters with his visiting mother (guest star Bonnie Bartlett).

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Tim and Al (Tim Allen, Richard Karn) fret over being stranded in a small-town airport on Christmas Eve.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Tim (Tim Allen) hires his laid-off brother Marty (guest star William O'Leary) to rebuild the "Tool Time" set.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Brad feels like he is second best when seventh-grader Randy begins taking classes at the high school.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Mike decides to give Vanessa a family-free holiday for Christmas; Ed accidentally shoots a bald eagle.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: When Eve is suspended from school for calling a boy a bad name, Mike is surprised name calling would be considered bullying.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Outdoor Man's longtime architect, Bill McKenzie (Richard Karn), faces professional competition from a young, attractive woman; Vanessa wonders if her recent promotion was based on looks or talent.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: When Vanessa hires a new housekeeper, the topic of immigration comes up, leading to Ed checking the green cards of Outdoor Man employees; Kristin finds a new way to make money.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: A battle of wits between Hyde and Kelso deteriorates into a wrestling match; Kitty goes to her first doctor's appointment for her pregnancy.

programme.desc: Donna gives Eric a big ugly ring as a symbol of their rekindled romance; Kitty is determined that menopause won't get her down.

programme.desc: Donna and Eric have a heart-to-heart discussion about their future after visiting different colleges.

programme.desc: Eric buys an engagement ring for Donna; Red gets menopausal Kitty a dog to lift her spirits.

programme.title: Man With a Plan

programme.desc: After Adam throws out his back on his 50th birthday, he is still determined to take a dirt bike excursion and check it off his bucket list; Don and Adam give out bonuses at work.

programme.title: Washington Week With The Atlantic
programme.desc: Peter Baker, The New York Times; Susan Glasser, The New Yorker; Idrees Kahloon, The Atlantic; Ashley Parker, The Atlantic.

programme.title: Firing Line With Margaret Hoover
programme.desc: Constitutional scholars Jeffrey Rosen and Yuval Levin discuss America's founding documents, why the ideas behind the Declaration of Independence and Constitution matter 250 years later, and the challenges that lie ahead for the American experiment.

programme.title: Great Performances
programme.sub-title: Now Hear This: The Iceland Sound
programme.desc: Uncovering how volcanoes and tight-knit communities inspire its artists.

programme.title: American Masters
programme.sub-title: Michael Tilson Thomas: Where Now Is
programme.desc: The life and career of Michael Tilson Thomas, music director of the San Francisco Symphony, founder and artistic director of the New World Symphony and conductor laureate of the London Symphony Orchestra.

programme.title: 10 Streets That Changed America
programme.desc: Tracing the 400-year evolution of Broadway in New York, from its beginnings as a Native American road to the electrically-lighted theater district known as the "Great White Way"; riding from Boston to New York on a dirt "highway."

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comic Brian Conley and broadcaster Nick Owen hunt for antiques in Shropshire and Worcestershire; assisting Nick is auctioneer Philip Serrell, while jewelry and gem expert Kate Bliss helps Brian.

programme.title: Mister Rogers' Neighborhood
programme.desc: Fred and Mr. McFeely visit a recycling center.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.sub-title: Pets and Pests; Go Fly a Kite
programme.desc: Arthur enlists the help of an expert mouse catcher; Binky, Muffy and Ladonna find a kite in the park.

programme.title: Wild Kratts
programme.desc: The Kratt brothers use a new archery skill to rescue baby animals from Donita Donata's yacht.

programme.title: Weather Hunters
programme.sub-title: Through Rain, Sun, Snow or Hail
